recent_coach_change: No recent change since the start of the WC cycle; Hassan was appointed in February 2024 (replacing Rui Vitoria) and has led the qualifying campaign.
qualification_path: Won CAF Group A unbeaten across all 10 matches (8 wins, 2 draws, 26 points, 20 goals for, 2 against, 7 clean sheets); sealed qualification with a 3-0 win away at Djibouti on October 8, 2025.
key_players: Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — captain and talisman, group's top scorer in qualifying with 9 goals
Omar Marmoush (Manchester City) — attacking partner to Salah and key goal threat
Mohamed El Shenawy (Al Ahly) — veteran goalkeeper behind a miserly qualifying defense
Trezeguet (Al Ahly) — experienced winger providing width and goals

injuries_doubts.issue: Nantes striker omitted from the final 26-man squad in favor of teenager Hamza Abdelkarim

squad_notes: Hossam Hassan confirmed his final 26-man squad on May 29, 2026. The biggest talking point was the call-up of 18-year-old Barcelona B prospect Hamza Abdelkarim, with Nantes striker Mostafa Mohamed left out. Salah captains a squad chasing Egypt's first-ever World Cup knockout berth.
warmup_assessment: Lost 2-1 to Brazil in Cleveland, Ohio on June 6, 2026 in their final warm-up; Salah came on at halftime. Egypt competed well and stretched Brazil but couldn't find an equalizer. Earlier 2025-26 form was mixed (Arab Cup group-stage exit; AFCON semi-final loss to Senegal).
shape_verdict: A dominant, defensively miserly qualifying campaign and the world-class Salah-Marmoush axis make Egypt dangerous, but mixed tournament form and a narrow loss to Brazil suggest they remain underdogs against Belgium.

news.headline: Salah double seals Egypt's 2026 World Cup qualification with 3-0 win at Djibouti
Hossam Hassan names final 26-man squad: teenager Hamza Abdelkarim in, Mostafa Mohamed out
Egypt lose 2-1 to Brazil in final World Cup warm-up; Salah featured in second half
